channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Simba and Azam FC victories tighten 2025/26 Tanzania Premier League title race
item.description: The race for the 2025/2026 Tanzania Mainland Premier League title is heating up, with Simba SC and Azam FC scoring important away wins that increase pressure on the defending champions, Young Africans...

item.title: Senior Maoist Leader Claims Arrest, Not Voluntary Surrender
item.description: A senior Maoist leader has publicly stated that he did not surrender voluntarily to the authorities but was instead arrested. This clarification comes amid conflicting reports that suggested his surre...

item.title: Inspector General Kanja Calls for Stronger Police Accountability and Service Improvements at County Commanders' Conference
item.description: Nairobi – On Thursday, Inspector General of Police Douglas Kanja officially opened the County Commanders' Conference at the National Police Leadership Academy. The gathering brought together senior of...

item.title: Akwa Ibom High Court Acquits Judiciary Accountant of N20 Million Theft Allegations Due to Insufficient Evidence
item.description: For nearly six years, Iniobong Essien, an Accountant II with the Akwa Ibom State Judiciary, dey face trial for alleged conspiracy and stealing of N20 million wey dem withdraw from Zenith Bank for offi...

item.title: CRDB Bank Launches Promo Sending 10 Customers to FIFA World Cup in US and Canada
item.description: CRDB Bank Tanzania imezindua kampeni mpya ya kibiashara iitwayo “Fainali Ndo Mpango na TemboCard Visa” ambayo inatoa fursa kwa wateja wake kushiriki droo ya kupata tiketi za kusafiri na kuangalia mech...

item.title: Envoi Allen, notable racehorse, dies at Cheltenham Festival after final race
item.description: During the 2026 Cheltenham Festival, tragedy struck as the 12-year-old racehorse Envoi Allen collapsed and died following his ninth-place finish in the Boodles Cheltenham Gold Cup. This event marks th...

item.title: US Launches Trade Investigation on 60 Economies Over Forced Labor Concerns
item.description: The United States has launched a new trade investigation that includes the UK, along with 59 other economies, to assess whether foreign governments have sufficiently addressed the use of forced labor ...

item.title: Adviser to White House Religious Liberty Commission resigns over removal of colleague and US military action in Iran
item.description: Sameerah Munshi, an adviser to the White House Religious Liberty Commission and its sole Muslim member, has resigned in protest against two key issues. Firstly, the removal of fellow commissioner Carr...

item.title: NUP urges House investigation into P1.8 billion DOH procurement issues
item.description: The National Unity Party (NUP) has expressed support for the initiative of its member, Quezon City 3rd District Rep. Franz Pumaren, to conduct a legislative investigation into the Department of Health...

item.title: Canadian Government Responds to Iranian Missile Attack on Domestic Forces
item.description: On March 1, 2026, Iran targeted the Canadian section of the Ali Al-Salem Air Base in Kuwait with a missile strike, an act that constitutes a direct threat to Canadian sovereignty. The Canadian governm...

item.title: NCERT textbook review process under scrutiny after judicial corruption chapter controversy
item.description: The recent controversy surrounding a Class 8 social science textbook, which was withdrawn shortly after its release due to a chapter on judicial corruption, has highlighted significant gaps in the NCE...

item.title: David Wenham Reflects on Acting, Life and Human Nature
item.description: Australian actor David Wenham, 60, reflects on his long and varied career as he revisits the site of the former Stables theatre in Darlinghurst, where he first made a name for himself in the controver...

item.title: Canada Celebrates Multiple Gold Medals at Milan Cortina Paralympics
item.description: Canada enjoyed a highly successful day at the Milan Cortina Paralympics as Natalie Wilkie captured her second gold medal and fourth overall in the women’s biathlon standing sprint pursuit. Representin...

item.title: Rwanda-Kenya Fintech Licensing Agreement to Ease Cross-Border Operations
item.description: A new fintech licensing framework between Rwanda and Kenya is set to make life easier for digital payment companies and fintech startups looking to expand regionally. Previously, firms had to apply fo...

item.title: Partial Flood Control Report May Be Archived Without Full Senate Signatures
item.description: Senate President Pro Tempore Panfilo 'Ping' Lacson said that the partial report of the Blue Ribbon Committee on the flood control controversy could be archived if it fails to gather the signatures of ...

item.title: Kenyan women recount abuse and harm linked to British Army operations
item.description: Three Kenyan women have publicly shared harrowing experiences involving British soldiers serving with the British Army Training Unit Kenya (BATUK), highlighting a longstanding pattern of misconduct. T...

item.title: Jamie Lee Curtis and Nicole Kidman Team Up for Scarpetta TV Series
item.description: Jamie Lee Curtis and Nicole Kidman have joined forces for 'Scarpetta', a new mystery crime series on Prime Video based on Patricia Cornwell's bestselling novels. Curtis, reflecting on her long Hollywo...

item.title: Overseas Filipino Workers Stranded in Saudi Arabia Repatriated via Singapore
item.description: Twenty-four overseas Filipino workers (OFWs) who were stranded in Saudi Arabia due to the escalating conflict in the Middle East have safely arrived in Singapore on a chartered flight. The Philippine ...

item.title: Digg restructures company, lays off staff, and discontinues app amid bot challenges
item.description: Digg, the link-sharing platform originally popularized by Kevin Rose, announced significant staff layoffs and the removal of its mobile app as part of a company-wide restructuring effort. Despite thes...

item.title: Teacher in Mara Region Receives Long-Delayed Transfer Allowances After Eight Years
item.description: After waiting for more than eight years, Samuel Magutu, a primary school teacher in Tarime District, Mara Region, has finally received Sh4.2 million in transfer allowances. The delay occurred when he ...
